run_summary: Portfolio holding 4 positions with modest unrealized gains (+1.8% to +4.2% each). NTLA, PTCT in original catalyst horizon; VECO a recent OVERDONE entry now flagged as RATIONAL sympathy (thesis intact but narrow). AXSM flagged as OVERDONE overshoot on light volume—already 24 hours into position, no new catalyst since entry. New flags include 2 re-entry attempts (AEIS, ALK) that fail the confidence bar, 1 fresh RATIONAL (VECO already held), and 1 new OVERDONE (ALK). Managing re-entry discipline strictly per instructions.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semi-cap equipment sympathy on NVDA/H200 China news
entries.thesis: VECO's move tracks the semi-cap equipment sympathy trade off NVDA H200 China news. No company-specific catalyst; magnitude in line with sector beta for a smaller-cap semi-cap name.
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a VECO-specific catalyst (contract win, analyst action) would reclassify; sector fade with VECO holding gains would also suggest something else.
entries.reasoning: VECO is already an open position (4 days held, +1.86%). New flag today re-describes the same holding as RATIONAL sympathy with semi-cap equipment peer cohort on NVDA/H200 China catalyst. This is a position re-evaluation, not a new trade signal. Thesis is already captured; no new buy signal warranted. Current holding is adequate; watching for sector continuation or divergence.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Oil spike on Hormuz toll headline + pre-earnings positioning (reports July 21)
entries.thesis: ALK is down sharply into an earnings print 8 days out, on a day when XLE +3% and Trump's Hormuz toll headline drove crude higher — classic jet-fuel-cost sympathy compounded by pre-earnings de-risking. The move looks larger than what the crude tape alone justifies for a hedged carrier, and airline earnings-week fear tends to overshoot then partially retrace once numbers land, unless guidance is genuinely bad.
entries.what_kills: If ALK is down materially more than peers, or if a specific negative pre-announcement/8-K surfaces before earnings, the sympathy read is wrong and this is company-specific.
entries.reasoning: ALK flagged as OVERDONE conf-3 on oil-spike pre-earnings overshoot thesis. CRITICAL: ALK was CLOSED 5 days ago at -4.25% loss. Exit reasoning cited: macro catalyst (Iran de-escalation → oil lower) did not produce expected move; ALK lagging peers. New flag cites DIFFERENT catalyst (Trump Hormuz toll headline driving crude spike NOW, plus pre-earnings positioning fear). However, this is a CONTINUATION of macro-driven airline positioning, not genuinely new information. The prior exit already rejected the airline-sector-sympathy thesis because ALK was lagging peers despite the macro setup. New flag does not address why ALK would not lag peers again on THIS crude spike. Confidence 3 triggers re-entry gate: must be conf >= 4. This flag is conf 3, so execution layer will downgrade to WATCH. Additionally, the new information bar is not met: oil-spike sympathy is the same class of thesis that was already tested and rejected (ALK lagging on macro catalysts). WATCH and require higher confidence + demonstrably new, ALK-specific catalyst (e.g., updated guidance, analyst note, fleet update) to consider re-entry.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semi-cap sympathy rebound with SMH +2.48%
entries.thesis: AEIS -13.2% over 5 days into today's +5.3% is a partial recovery on light volume (0.69x). If the prior week's selloff was tied to a peer-driven derating without a company-specific catalyst, the bounce has room to run as SMH breaks out. The subdued volume suggests forced sellers are gone but buyers haven't fully returned.
entries.what_kills: A delayed 8-K reveals a company-specific reason for the 5-day weakness (guide cut, customer loss); SMH rolls over
entries.reasoning: AEIS flagged as UNDERDONE conf-3 on semi-cap rebound after 5-day -13.21% drawdown. CRITICAL: AEIS was CLOSED 1 day ago at -4.43% loss. Exit reasoning: 'entry was at peak drawdown, exit into today's partial bounce.' Today's flag shows exactly that bounce (+5.32% one-day move after the -13.21% decline). This is the exact retrace the prior exit anticipated and exited into. New flag is effectively saying 'the retrace I exited into is now happening'—that is NOT new information; it is the planned unfolding of the thesis that was already rejected. Confidence 3 triggers re-entry gate: must be conf >= 4. This flag is conf 3, so execution layer will downgrade to WATCH. Additionally, no genuinely new catalyst exists: the prior exit already understood that 5-day weakness without an 8-K implied forced selling and a bounce was likely. Re-entering on the bounce itself is performance-chasing the exit signal. WATCH and require either (a) conf >= 4 with new 8-K or peer-specific catalyst, or (b) a second day of continuation at volume higher than 0.69x to suggest institutional re-entry.

entries.name: Perdoceo Education Corporation
entries.catalyst: Possible reaction to today's PSLF rule-change reporting, but no direct company 8-K
entries.thesis: Move is likely policy-driven — CNBC ran a piece today on new Public Service Loan Forgiveness rules, and the for-profit education sector is highly sensitive to federal student-aid and forgiveness policy. But without a specific 8-K, guidance change, or explicit rule affecting Perdoceo's schools, I can't judge whether the magnitude is justified. Could be legitimate reaction to a broader Title IV / gainful-employment rule change I'm not seeing, or an overreaction to a tangential PSLF headline.
entries.what_kills: If peers are flat/up, this is Perdoceo-specific (guidance leak, enrollment miss preview) and the policy thesis is wrong; would then need company-specific evidence

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings pending in the flag window
entries.thesis: Move is bigger than a pure sympathy-with-semis explanation warrants, but volume is only 1.1x average — suggests something specific but not broadly noticed. Could be a broker downgrade, a channel-check note, or a read-through from a peer's negative preannouncement. Without an identifiable catalyst, I can't credibly call direction.
entries.what_kills: If a private company announcement (competitor win/loss) explains it, or if a customer concentration disclosure surfaces — could be either direction

entries.name: Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated
entries.catalyst: No specific catalyst; sector/rates-driven
entries.thesis: Move looks consistent with a broader rates/CRE bid — XLF +0.65%, KRE +1.1%, VIX -5%. JLL is a beta play on commercial transaction volumes, so it tends to move harder than the CRE index on positive rate/credit news. Without a specific catalyst it reads as sector-driven and appropriately sized, not a mispricing.
entries.what_kills: If peers are flat/down, JLL has a specific catalyst (M&A rumor, mandate win) and the RATIONAL read undersells it

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semi-equipment sympathy amid SMH weakness
entries.thesis: Move looks like semi-cap-equipment sympathy in a broad semis drawdown. Onto's five-day change is still positive (+4.5%), so this is a single-day pullback in an otherwise firm name, not a breakdown. Magnitude is roughly 3x SMH, reasonable for a higher-beta semi-equipment stock. Not a mispricing on the evidence available.
entries.what_kills: If peers are only down 1-2%, ONTO has a company-specific issue (customer concentration read-through from IBM or similar); would flip toward OVERDONE candidate

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Pre-earnings positioning; upcoming earnings July 28
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with pre-earnings positioning in a low-priced, high-beta name on a day with VIX compressing and small caps bid (IWM +0.4%). Volume only 1.17x average argues against a specific catalyst. Reads as sector/positioning noise rather than a mispricing.
entries.what_kills: If airlines are mixed/down and JBLU is a solo mover, there's a specific catalyst (route news, activist, partnership) worth chasing

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly Iran/Hormuz escalation read-through to drone/unmanned defense; possibly contract rumor
entries.thesis: KTOS is up sharply while the defense ETF ITA is flat and market is broadly risk-on. Iran/Hormuz escalation news (Trump announcing blockade of Iranian shipping) is a plausible driver for defense names with drone/unmanned systems exposure, but if it were a sector-wide catalyst ITA would be up. Could be a contract award rumor, analyst action, or Iran-specific drone/tactical read-through. Cannot articulate a directional call without knowing which.
entries.what_kills: N/A — this is genuinely unclear pending catalyst discovery.

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Unknown — sustained multi-day decline suggests a company-specific negative event within the last week not captured in current signals
entries.thesis: PTCT is extending losses in a weak healthcare tape (XLV -1.9%), but the five-day decline suggests a specific negative catalyst earlier in the week that we don't have in signals. Without knowing whether that catalyst was a trial readout, regulatory setback, or something reversible, we can't call whether today's continued decline is capitulation (fadeable) or continued digestion of bad fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: N/A — genuinely unclear pending catalyst identification.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: No identifiable catalyst in filings or news; ITA weakness alone doesn't explain magnitude
entries.thesis: Down materially again today after already dropping ~20% over five sessions, with no 8-K, no earnings scheduled, and only modest ITA weakness (-1.7%). The persistence and magnitude suggest something company-specific (contract loss, program cancellation, or leaked guidance concern), but I cannot identify it from available signals. Neither a bull (oversold bounce) nor bear (further downside) case reaches conf 3 without knowing what's driving it.
entries.what_kills: If a benign explanation surfaces (index rebalance flow, block sale, sympathy with a defense peer's guide-down), the slide loses signal value and the setup dissolves.

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Semi sector selloff (SMH -4.16%) tied to AI-trade unwind and ORCL -6.4%
entries.thesis: LSCC down ~5% on a day SMH is -4.2%, NVDA -3.5%, AVGO -4%, ONTO -5.2% — this is beta, not signal. No 8-K, no earnings pending. The magnitude is entirely appropriate to what's happening in semis today and the 5-day return is flat, meaning this is one-day tape action.
entries.what_kills: If LSCC continues to underperform SMH in coming sessions without a broader semi recovery, look for a name-specific catalyst.

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — moves against sector on above-avg volume with no visible news
entries.thesis: FIZZ down ~8% on 1.64x volume, on a day XLP is +0.55% — this is a name-specific move against the sector, not sympathy. But there's no 8-K, no earnings, and no news in the feed. Founder-controlled float with historically thin liquidity makes single-holder selling or a private analyst note plausible. Cannot articulate direction at conf 3 without more information.
entries.what_kills: If it's simply a block trade or algorithmic dislocation in a thin float, there's no thesis to have.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
entries.catalyst: XBI -2.33% and continued rotation out of high-beta clinical biotech; VIX +13%
entries.thesis: CRSP -5.1% and BEAM -5.1% together on a day XBI is -2.3% is a gene-editing subgroup move roughly 2x sector beta — reasonable for high-beta clinical-stage names in a risk-off tape. Five-day drawdowns of -18% and -12% suggest ongoing sector rotation out of speculative biotech, not a single-day event. No 8-Ks.
entries.what_kills: A single-name clinical readout or FDA action would recategorize this as company-specific.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI selloff and gene-editing sub-sector weakness
entries.thesis: BEAM is down materially on essentially average volume (1.09x) with no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, and no company-specific news surfaced. The move is consistent with high-beta gene-editing names amplifying an XBI down-day; CRSP moving similarly reinforces this is a sub-sector unwind, not idiosyncratic. Magnitude looks proportional to XBI beta.
entries.what_kills: If a BEAM-specific pipeline setback (e.g., clinical hold, trial pause) emerges in filings later today or tomorrow — that would reclassify as UNDERDONE-down.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Profit-taking on recent run compounded by XBI selloff
entries.thesis: BBIO is down today but still up substantially over five sessions. Elevated volume (1.32x) fits a partial profit-take on a name that ran hard, amplified by XBI weakness. Magnitude looks proportional given both the sector down-day and the extended prior run.
entries.what_kills: If BBIO gives back the entire five-day gain and keeps going, an Attruby (acoramidis) commercial or competitive concern (BMY Camzyos, PFE tafamidis dynamics) may be surfacing.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Possible Iran de-escalation / defense multiple compression, but unconfirmed
entries.thesis: AVAV is down 5.9% today and 13.9% over 5 days while ITA is flat and SPY +0.85%. Iran de-escalation headlines pressure defense demand narrative, and Anduril CEO comments about IPO 'hype cycle' may be pulling drone/autonomy multiples lower. But without a company-specific 8-K, the weight of Iran ceasefire vs. Anduril commentary vs. some unseen catalyst is not clearly one-sided enough for a directional call.
entries.what_kills: Move is purely Iran-ceasefire sympathy — mean-reverts once headlines fade; peer KTOS/RCAT show similar magnitude

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown gene-editing subgroup event
entries.thesis: NTLA -8.3% and CRSP -4.8% together while XBI +0.8% indicates a gene-editing subgroup catalyst — possibly a safety/data event at one of the CRISPR programs or a competitive readout. Move magnitude is consistent with a mid-severity clinical concern spread across the two most correlated names.
entries.what_kills: Isolated NTLA-specific pipeline setback — would make CRSP move sympathy-only and NTLA move justified

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide housing/rate weakness; Fed minutes showing hawkish split
entries.thesis: BLDR is down sharply over five days with no 8-K and average volume today — the move looks like continuation of a rate-driven housing derating (Fed minutes showed split on cuts, XLRE -1.65%, XLB -2.6%). The magnitude over 5 days is large but proportional to a housing demand scare; today's leg is just tape-following. Nothing here suggests mispricing either direction.
entries.what_kills: A specific negative company catalyst (guide cut, 8-K) emerging would recharacterize this as UNDERDONE bearish; conversely a rate-cut surprise would make it OVERDONE

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly sector rotation or analyst note not in feed
entries.thesis: ALGN is down sharply on a day when XLV is -1.3% and XLY is -1.78% — some sector explanation but the magnitude exceeds sector move. Volume is BELOW average, however, which argues against a discovered catalyst; more like thin-tape reaction. No 8-K. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ — bear case needs a whisper/note we don't have; bull case (fade the move) needs a reason it's overdone that isn't just 'no news.'
entries.what_kills: Discovery of a specific negative catalyst (guide-down, competitive threat news) would break the UNCLEAR framing toward RATIONAL bearish
